mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month through lower cost of capital and higher present value of cash flows.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.2% within a single month, it signals a sudden cooling in the labor market that historically precedes a recessionary earnings contraction, driving negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising core inflation and a tightening monetary stance compresses consumer credit availability, leading to a demand shock that suppresses corporate revenues and equity valuations over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When the Industrial Production Index exceeds 105.0 while housing starts remain below 1.4 million units, it indicates a supply-demand imbalance favoring industrial over residential sectors, which historically leads to positive equity returns over a one-week horizon as capital flows into cyclical stocks.
failure_condition: The signal produces a negative predictive rank IC over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 5-day returns do not differ significantly from the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a sustained month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls signals robust labor demand that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with forward SPX returns during the specified regime, or if the out-of-sample Sharpe ratio of a long-only strategy based on this signal drops below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential quarterly increase in real GDP reflects expanding aggregate demand that directly lifts corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-quarter horizon regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward 60-day returns in quarters with GDP growth above 2% are not statistically higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of a federal funds rate above 5.0% and an unemployment rate below 3.8% creates a high-cost, tight-labor environment that squeezes corporate profit margins, leading to negative equity returns over a one-week horizon as valuation multiples contract.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction coefficient in a panel regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates by more than 0.5 percentage points quarter-over-quarter while industrial production remains flat, it signals a demand-led slowdown that historically precedes equity drawdowns over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 qoq change < -0.5% and INDPRO change < 0.1%
failure_condition: The signal produces positive forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the predictive information ratio over a 5-year walk-forward test falls below 0.1.
